metadata.description: Micke Berg's personal Claude Code plugins. Cross-platform, no company specifics.

plugins.name: git-tools
plugins.description: Git workflow skills: a conventional-commit skill (draft, approve, commit) and a GitHub PR skill (build a PR from your commits and open it with gh).

plugins.name: guardrails
plugins.description: A verify skill that runs your project's own checks (lint, typecheck, test, build) and reports honestly, plus an optional off-by-default pre-push gate hook.

plugins.name: recap
plugins.description: Summaries from your git history. Starts with a standup skill that turns your recent commits into a glanceable, spoken-style standup cheat sheet.

plugins.name: setup-kit
plugins.description: The delivery toolkit for a professional Claude Code setup: setup-audit (inventory a machine vs a target state), install (idempotent installer, verified by re-running the audit), init-team (interview a repo into a tailored CLAUDE.md + permissions baseline), and team-baseline (grade a repo against a professional baseline). Ships CLAUDE.md templates, a settings baseline, and a delivery runbook.

plugins.name: humanizer
plugins.description: Removes signs of AI-generated writing from text (em dash overuse, rule of three, inflated symbolism, vague attributions, AI vocabulary, and more), then reshapes it to your voice via one editable Customize block. Based on Wikipedia's "Signs of AI writing" guide.
